title: Engine observability — logging policy + CI gate + motion smoothness

stories.title: Motion smoothness — frame-time probe + GridMover stutter fix
stories.summary: Build a deterministic Playwright probe that measures frame-delta jitter while the player walks across the Kaboom Crew arena. Capture a histogram of frame durations + per-cell tween dt deltas. Investigate whether GridMover's cell-tween + lane-assist introduce visible micro-stutters (cell boundary snaps, recompute order, BatchingSystem invalidation churn). Land a targeted fix if root cause is in the renderer/scheduler/tween path; if it turns out to be macOS PSU/throttling, document the workaround.
verification: tests/e2e/motion-smoothness.spec.ts captures ≥3 s of frame-time samples while gotoCell drives the player across ≥5 cells, asserts P95 frame-delta ≤ N ms on the smoke project
before/after metrics in the PR body (median frame ms, P95 frame ms, max stall ms)

headed Playwright probe tests/e2e/motion-smoothness.spec.ts samples rAF deltas + per-frame Transform.position while gotoCell drives the player across 7 cells; asserts frame P95 ≤ 25 ms, motion CV ≤ 50%, zeroSteps ≤ 5% of frames
baseline (before fix): meanStep=0.0330 stdev=0.0034 CV=10.2% zeroSteps=1 / 207 frames — one cell-boundary stall per crossing

after carry-over fix in engine/core/systems/grid-movement-system.ts: meanStep=0.0335 stdev=0.0031 CV=9.4% zeroSteps=0 — overshoot now feeds the next tween instead of resetting to lerp=0 on cell boundaries
unit test 'carries lerp overshoot across cell boundaries' guards the new code path; 631 → 632 unit tests passing

stories.title: Logging policy doc — severity scale + diagnostics-vs-console rules
stories.summary: Write `docs/diagnostics-policy.md` capturing the severity scale (fatal/error/warn/info/debug/trace), when-to-use-diagnostics-vs-console, the structured-payload schema (domain_verb code + JSON context), and the rules for engine vs example code. Source: notes/logging-policy-thinking.md.
verification: docs/diagnostics-policy.md exists; referenced from CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md
docs/diagnostics-policy.md created (8 sections: severity scale, where-to-emit, code naming, per-system debug, lifecycle events, CI gates, why-not-console, migration playbook)
CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md Read-First sections reference the new policy

stories.title: CI gate: ban raw console.log in engine/** + examples/**/src/**
stories.summary: scripts/check-no-console-log.mjs walks the configured globs and rejects bare console.{log,warn,error}. Allowlist via `// agf-allow:console <reason>` markers. Wire into `npm run repo:hygiene` (already part of preflight) so CI fails when someone slips a log in.
verification: script flags an introduced console.log; passes on the current tree after audit
preflight runs the gate before typecheck

scripts/check-no-console-log.mjs walks git-tracked .ts/.tsx/.mjs in engine/** and examples/**/src/**, rejects bare console.{log,warn,error,info,debug,trace}; allowlist via '// agf-allow:console <reason>' on the call line or a previous non-blank line (≤ 3 above)
exposed as 'npm run check:console-log'; unit test in tests/unit/check-no-console-log.test.ts shells out and asserts the documented exit codes
preflight wiring deferred to AGF-LOG-AUDIT-ENGINE (gate currently reports 48 violations against the tree; wiring to repo:hygiene before the audit pass would block the sprint branch)

stories.title: Audit + purge existing console.* in engine/** under the new policy
stories.summary: Walk every `console.*` call site inside engine/** and either route it through the diagnostics channel, downgrade to debug-flag gated, or add an `agf-allow:` marker with a one-line rationale. Goal: a clean baseline before the CI gate flips on.
verification: check-no-console-log.mjs passes against engine/** with zero unmarked sites
PR diff lists every conversion

stories.title: Named per-system debug loggers + AGF_DEBUG flag
stories.summary: Engine adds a `createDebugLogger(name)` factory that returns a logger gated by a project-wide `AGF_DEBUG` env / query-param. Systems opt-in (`const log = createDebugLogger('grid-movement')`) so debug output can be filtered by name and stays silent by default.
verification: unit test: logger is no-op when AGF_DEBUG is unset; emits when set to '*' or matching name
at least one engine system (grid-movement) consumes the new logger
engine/runtime/diagnostics/debug-logger.ts exports createDebugLogger(name, { bus, selector }) — no-op unless AGF_DEBUG selector matches the source name AND a diagnostics bus is wired
AGF_DEBUG is read from process.env in Node, globalThis.__AGF_DEBUG__ override (for tests), or ?agf_debug=… on location in the browser; '*' / single name / comma-separated allowlist syntax
DIAGNOSTIC_SEVERITIES extended with 'debug' + 'trace' per docs/diagnostics-policy.md; emitted events carry severity='debug' or 'trace'
8 unit tests across parseDebugSelector (5) + createDebugLogger (4) including no-op / disabled / debug emission / trace emission; 632 → 640 unit tests passing

stories.title: Structured lifecycle traces — scene load / system register / runtime stop
stories.summary: Emit one structured trace per lifecycle event (scene load start/end with entity counts, system register/unregister, runtime stop) through the existing diagnostics channel. No console.log path. Helps post-mortems on hangs + ghost-entity bugs.
verification: diagnostics buffer captures `agf.scene.loaded`, `agf.system.registered`, `agf.runtime.stopped` payloads in an integration test
SystemScheduler accepts optional { diagnostics } and emits AGF_SCHEDULER_SYSTEM_REGISTERED / _DEREGISTERED on register/unregister; legacy callers without a bus stay silent
CommandQueue accepts optional { diagnostics } and emits AGF_SCENE_LOAD_APPLIED with { entityCountBefore, entityCountAfter, sceneId } when draining a scene.load
engine/runtime/start.ts emits AGF_RUNTIME_STARTED on startup (with systemCount) and AGF_RUNTIME_STOPPED on stop()
+6 unit tests (scheduler register/unregister/silent-unknown/no-bus + command-queue scene.load/no-emit-for-other-commands); 640 → 646 unit tests passing

stories.title: engine doctor reports diagnostics noise summary
stories.summary: Add a `diagnostics` section to `engine doctor` that summarises total events by severity + top-N codes since boot. Helps spot a system that's spam-warning every frame without grep-ing console.
verification: engine doctor --json prints a `diagnostics: { total, bySeverity, topCodes }` block on a sample project

stories.title: Boot noise budget — fail when startup emits more than N logs
stories.summary: Preflight smoke test asserts that booting hello-3d emits ≤ N (small, e.g. 5) info-level diagnostics before idle. Catches regressions where someone adds a per-frame log to a startup path.
verification: smoke e2e fails when a deliberate noisy log is added; passes on the cleaned tree

stories.title: Detect + log entity recreation regressions
stories.summary: When `entity.create` lands an id that was deleted earlier in the same session, emit a `agf.entity.recreated` warning with both call stacks (when stack traces are cheap). Root-cause aid for ghost-entity bugs like the S082 restart issue.
verification: unit test: recreating a previously-deleted id triggers the warning; same-frame ids don't
CommandQueue tracks deletedIds across entity.delete + scene.load wipes; emits AGF_ENTITY_RECREATED warning when a later entity.create lands a previously-deleted id
+4 unit tests (delete→create, fresh create no warn, scene.load wipe→recreate warn, legitimate scene.load reload doesn't warn); 646 → 651 unit tests passing

stories.title: Engine renderer probe — `runtime.renderer.inspect()` + CLI
stories.summary: First-class engine tool: `runtime.renderer.inspect()` returns a compact JSON dump of the Three.js scene tree (Mesh/InstancedMesh/Light counts + visibility), BatchingSystem bucket records (key, capacity, live, lastWorld memo) and MeshHandleRegistry entries. `engine probe <projectDir>` exposes the same via the CLI. The ad-hoc probe used during the S082 restart bug becomes a one-curl primitive.
verification: unit test: inspect() output schema validates against schemas/renderer-inspect.schema.json
live probe: `curl /__agf/renderer-inspect` returns non-empty bucket + handle data on kaboom-crew

stories.title: Investigate why Vite manualChunks doesn't split three.webgpu
stories.summary: The S70 WEBGPU-lazy-import story split `three/webgpu` into a dynamic import + manualChunks rule for `three-webgpu-*`. The split never produces a separate chunk in practice — Vite folds the WebGPU code straight back into the main `three-*` chunk (~520 KB). Investigate why (dynamic import shape, module specifier, manualChunks ordering) and ship a fix that actually code-splits, then tighten the budget back from 560 KB toward the original 340 KB.
verification: build produces a `three-webgpu-*` chunk in `dist/assets/` for kaboom-crew (which doesn't use WebGPU)
scripts/check-bundle-size.mjs three- budget lowered to ≤ 400 KB and passes
